top_candidates.reasoning: The engineering layer opens with 'Therefore — and the conjunction is load-bearing' which is the explicit recognition of tasmāt as 2.68's structural connector to 2.67. The cross-verse pattern of pratiṣṭhitā (2.55, 2.57, 2.58, 2.61, 2.68) is named verbatim. The sarvaśaḥ totality-qualifier is preserved as 'on every side, sarvaśaḥ' in the body. These three anchors together identify 2.68 specifically — no other Gita verse runs this exact constellation of features.

Adjacent verse with the prior pratiṣṭhitā formulation: 'tāni sarvāṇi saṁyamya yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ / vaśe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā'. Shares the prajñā pratiṣṭhitā compound but lacks the post-2.67 restatement function that the tasmāt in 2.68 carries. The engineering layer's opening explicitly invokes the conjunction-as-restatement, which 2.61 does not have.

BG 2.58 (the tortoise verse) also contains the indriyāṇi indriya-arthebhyaḥ phrasing and has prajñā pratiṣṭhitā as the closing predicate. But 2.58's specific contribution is the tortoise metaphor (kūrmaḥ), which is not present in this analog. Distant possibility.
